message:
fix(telemetry): shadow storage timestamp filtering, stats, naming, and cleanup

Four correctness fixes in the shadow observation archive:

1. Event timestamp filtering: the retention filter was checking the
   observation-level timestamp instead of individual event timestamps,
   causing events within a valid observation to be incorrectly retained
   or pruned.  Fix: filter on each event's own timestamp.

2. Stats tracking: unique_pids and unique_identities counters were
   computed lazily at report time, missing events added between
   reports.  Fix: update both counters eagerly on each record() call.

3. File naming: observation files used second-precision timestamps,
   causing collisions when multiple observations occurred within the
   same second.  Fix: extract create_unique_observation_file helper
   using microsecond precision plus a suffix counter.

4. Archive cleanup: stale file cleanup did not recurse into per-pid
   subdirectories, leaving empty directories after their files
   expired.  Fix: extract recursive cleanup_archive_path helper that
   removes expired files and prunes empty subdirectories.

Four new unit tests cover the fixed behaviors.  One new integration
test (shadow_cleanup_removes_expired_nested_archive_files) verifies
nested cleanup and empty directory removal.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
